description: Pakistani Islamist jihadist militant organization founded in 1985–1986 by Hafiz Saeed and others with funding from Osama bin Laden. Its primary objective is to merge Kashmir with Pakistan and seeks destruction of India, Hinduism, and Judaism through jihad. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it has carried out major attacks in India including the 2008 Mumbai attacks. It is supported by Pakistan's ISI and operates primarily in Kashmir.

country_links.evidence_quote: The LeT was created to participate in the Mujahideen conflict against the Najibullah regime in Afghanistan . In the process, the outfit developed deep linkages with Afghanistan and has several Afghan nationals in its cadre.
country_links.context: LeT has Afghan nationals in its cadre, operated in Afghanistan, and directed attacks against Indian targets there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Active regions: Worldwide, predominantly in the Indian subcontinent Pakistan , India , Afghanistan , Australia , United States , Bangladesh , Nepal

News sources have reported that members of LeT were planning to attack the U.S. and Indian embassies in Dhaka, Bangladesh, on 26 November 2009, to coincide with the one-year anniversary of the November 2008 Mumbai attacks.
country_links.context: LeT members planned to attack the U.S. and Indian embassies in Dhaka, Bangladesh.

country_links.evidence_quote: LeT members also have been reported to have engaged in conflicts in the Philippines , Bosnia
country_links.context: LeT members have been reported to have engaged in conflicts in Bosnia.

country_links.evidence_quote: David Headley ... performed surveillance missions in Mumbai to scout targets for the 2008 Mumbai attacks , and in Copenhagen, Denmark to help plot an attack against the Danish newspaper Jyllands-Posten , which had published cartoons of Muhammad .
country_links.context: LeT operative David Headley conducted surveillance in Copenhagen, Denmark to plot an attack against the Danish newspaper Jyllands-Posten.

country_links.evidence_quote: It has been designated as a terrorist group by the United Nations and numerous other countries and been responsible for terrorist attacks on civilians in India, such as the 2000 Red Fort attack , 2005 Delhi bombings , 2006 Mumbai train bombings , 2008 Mumbai attacks and the 2025 Pahalgam attack .
country_links.context: LeT carries out attacks and operations in India, including the 2008 Mumbai attacks and the 2025 Pahalgam attack.

country_links.evidence_quote: Abu Saifullah Khalid, also known as Razaullah Nizamani, the mastermind of the 2006 attack on the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh headquarters in Nagpur, who headed the LeT’s operations from Nepal since 2000, was reportedly killed by three gunmen in Pakistan’s Sindh province on 18 May 2025.
country_links.context: LeT used Nepal as a transit route and had a module there headed by Abu Saifullah Khalid.

country_links.evidence_quote: LeT members also have been reported to have engaged in conflicts in the Philippines
country_links.context: LeT members have been reported to have engaged in conflicts in the Philippines.

country_links.evidence_quote: Mahmoud Mohamed Ahmed Bahaziq – the leader of LeT in Saudi Arabia and one of its financiers.
country_links.context: Lashkar-e-Taiba has a leader and financier in Saudi Arabia, and a senior leader traveled to Jeddah to request financial support from Osama bin Laden.

country_links.evidence_quote: The LeT also collected donations from the Pakistani immigrant community in the Persian Gulf and United Kingdom, Islamic Non-Governmental Organizations, and Pakistani and Kashmiri businessmen.
country_links.context: LeT collected donations from the Pakistani immigrant community in the United Kingdom.

country_links.evidence_quote: A group of men dubbed the Virginia Jihad Network attended LeT training camps and were convicted in 2006 of conspiring to provide material support to the LeT.
country_links.context: LeT provided training to U.S. citizens who later conspired to support LeT, and its headquarters was used as a hideout for perpetrators of attacks in the U.S.
